The time zone. This is the same as the time zone you specified when you configured the NSS feed.

Description: The epoch time of the transaction
The name of the rule that was applied to the DNS request
The name of the action that was applied to the DNS request
The name of the rule that was applied to the DNS response
The name of the action that was applied to the DNS response
The name of the EDNS Client Subnet (ECS) rule that was applied to the DNS transaction
The name of the DNS Gateway rule
Indicates if the DNS transaction uses TCP

The IP address of the user. This can be the internal IP address if it is visible (e.g., traffic sent through a GRE tunnel or an internal IP address indicated using XFF). Otherwise, it's the client's internet (NATed Public) IP address.
The duration of the DNS request in milliseconds
The server IP address of the request
The unique record identifier for each log
Insights Logs: This field is specific to NSS

Description: The path of the packet capture (PCAP) file that captured the transaction. The PCAP ID has the following format: <Company ID>/<Directory>/<PCAP File Name>. The company ID is the internal ID of an organization and can be found on the Company Profile page. The directory is the log type. To download the PCAP file, go to the Capture column on the DNS Insights Logs page.
The gateway location or sub-location of the source
The Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN) in the DNS request
The resolved IP or NAME in the DNS response
The category of the content of the DNS request
The category of the content of the DNS response
The DNS request type
The DNS response type. The means or format of the response.
The server port of the request
Indicates if the characters specified in the Feed Escape Character field of the NSS configuration page were hex encoded
Insights Logs: This field is specific to NSS

Description: The DNS error code. Usually an incomplete or failed transaction.
The EDNS Client Subnet (ECS) prefix used in the DNS request. This field displays a numeric string.
The DNS Gateway server protocol
Flags indicating the DNS Gateway status for the transaction
Example:
PRIMARY_SERVER_RESPONSE_PASS (i.e., Primary Server Attempted)
SECONDARY_SERVER_RESPONSE_PASS (i.e., Secondary Server Attempted)
FO_DEST_PASS (i.e., Query Forwarded to Destination)
FO_DEST_ERR (i.e., Error Response Returned to Client)
FO_DEST_DROP (i.e., Query Dropped)
None

The type of DNS tunnel or network application
The protocol type
The login name in email address format
The department
The company name
